codeReview.summary: The recent-window bridge fix and Riot-proxy diagnostics are implemented, but the feature still does not complete or prove the required DB-first backfill/update behavior in the actual returning-player flow. The dashboard only refreshes stored matches after newly inserted rows, and the handoff evidence never shows the DB-backed view after reconciliation, so VAL-FRESHNESS-001 and VAL-FRESHNESS-002 are not fully satisfied.

issues.description: After `syncNewHeadMatchesFromRiot` completes, the UI only re-fetches `/api/stored-matches` when `syncResult.analyzedCount > 0`. If reconciliation only refreshes stale recent rows via `refreshedStaleCount`, the DB-first view keeps showing pre-refresh data even though the feature requires the refreshed returning-player view to reflect reconciled DB state.

The live call site still hardcodes `{ windowSize: 25, ... }` and never passes or reads a bounded `recentWindowSize` from the new freshness metadata. That means the app is not actually wiring the intended bounded recent-window reconciliation behavior through the real returning-player path; it is relying on the bridge default instead of the tracked metadata introduced for this milestone.

The bridge now analyzes missing recent Riot IDs, but the reviewed evidence stops at comparing pre-refresh `/api/stored-matches` output to `/api/match-history`. There is no end-to-end proof in the implementation or handoff that those missing recent matches were backfilled into `LeagueBack` and then surfaced through the DB-first route without duplicates, which is the core requirement of VAL-FRESHNESS-002.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission shared state does not currently record any stable returning-player validation account or the expected live evidence pattern for freshness backfill. The worker had to rediscover that `Bumsdito#3005` was a useful target and manually compare Supabase plus local API responses to assess the feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for session `c2854830-1900-4d41-bff6-34ac99753f33` shows several custom Supabase/API inspection commands before validation, and neither `AGENTS.md` nor `.factory/library/user-testing.md` names a reusable returning-player fixture/account for data-freshness work.

summary: Review failed: the commit improves recent-head reconciliation logic and Riot-proxy error details, but it does not complete or prove the required DB-first backfill/update behavior for returning players, so the VAL-FRESHNESS-001/002 intent remains unmet.
